title: Narcissus loiseleurii
taxonomy: Narcissus loiseleurii is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, in the family Amaryllidaceae. It belongs to the genus Narcissus and is commonly known as the Loesel's orchid or Loesel's narcissus. This classification puts Narcissus loiseleurii in the same family as other popular flowers like the daffodil and the snowdrop. Further classification places it in the order Asparagales, which includes many other flowering plants. At the species level, Narcissus loiseleurii is considered a rare and endemic species found in Europe.
uses: Narcissus loiseleurii has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Its bulbs contain alkaloids and glycosides, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Traditional remedies have used the plant to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. However, further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Narcissus loiseleurii for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to fully understand its potential medicinal applications.

intro: Narcissus loiseleurii, also known as Loesel's or Lusus Bellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus loiseleurii include alkaloids like galantamine and homocholine, which have been found to possess neuroprotective and cholinesterase-inhibiting properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Narcissus loiseleurii is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
